normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def InsertSpec (t : BST α) (k : α) : Prop :=
  (∀ x, x ∈ (insert t k) ↔ (x ∈ t ∨ x = k)) ∧
  (∀ x, x ∈ t → x ∈ insert t k) ∧
  (k ∈ t → insert t k = t) ∧
  (¬ (k ∈ t) → k ∈ insert t k) ∧
  (∀ x, x ≠ k → (x ∈ t ↔ x ∈ insert t k))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: State the intended membership behavior: inserting k adds k if absent, otherwise leaves the tree unchanged; all other keys preserve membership.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → StrictSortedInorder t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input tree is a valid BST whose in-order traversal is strictly ascending (equivalently, no duplicates and BST ordering holds).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → IsBST (insert t k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the algorithm returns a BST when given a BST.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ t : BST α, StrictSortedInorder t → StrictSortedInorder (insert t k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the in-order traversal of the output remains strictly sorted.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
termination_by insert t k => size t

-- and/or
-- measure decreases on recursive calls
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show the implementation terminates (e.g., by decreasing tree size on recursive calls).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t : BST α, IsBST t →
  (k = rootKey t → insert t k = t) ∧
  (k < rootKey t → insert t k = mkNode (rootKey t) (insert (leftSub t) k) (rightSub t)) ∧
  (k > rootKey t → insert t k = mkNode (rootKey t) (leftSub t) (insert (rightSub t) k))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Match the algorithm’s behavior to BST insertion logic: equality returns unchanged; less-than recurses left; greater-than recurses right.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → ∀ x : α,
  x ∈ insert t k → (x ∈ t ∨ x = k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If a key appears in the output, it must have been in the input or be the inserted key.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → ∀ x : α,
  (x ∈ t ∨ x = k) → x ∈ insert t k
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Every key from the input remains present, and the inserted key is present when appropriate.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → k ∈ t → insert t k = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key already exists, insertion is a no-op (tree unchanged).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → ¬ (k ∈ t) → k ∈ insert t k
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key is not present, the output contains the new key.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t : BST α, IsBST t → ∀ x : α,
  x ≠ k → (x ∈ insert t k ↔ x ∈ t)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: All keys other than k have exactly the same membership in input and output.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ t : BST α, IsBST t →
  (k ∈ t → size (keys (insert t k)) = size (keys t)) ∧
  (¬ (k ∈ t) → size (keys (insert t k)) = size (keys t) + 1)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Quantify the effect on the number of stored keys: unchanged if k exists; otherwise exactly one new node is added.
